plugin_desc: Mellanox (NVIDIA) Spectrum Ethernet switch SNMP collection (Onyx / MLNX-OS). Beyond standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it adds device CPU utilization via HOST-RESOURCES hrProcessorLoad (averaged across cores), which the generic SNMP switch plugin does not provide. Memory is exposed only through the multi-row hrStorage table and temperature/fan/power only through the ENTITY-SENSOR table (sensor types mixed in one table), neither row/type-filterable by the SNMP input, so those are N/A. Supported brands/models — Mellanox/NVIDIA Spectrum Ethernet switches running Onyx or MLNX-OS (SN2000/SN3000/SN4000 series). InfiniBand-only fabrics are out of scope.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: CPU utilization of the Mellanox Spectrum switch (HOST-RESOURCES hrProcessorLoad, averaged across cores).

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
